86.reason: [The condition `type(c) != 'a' || type(c) != '0'` is always true because any character is either not 'a' or not '0'. This prevents proper word extraction. It should be `type(c) == 'a'` to continue reading letters.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The condition `type(c) != 'a' || type(c) != '0'` is always true because any character is either not 'a' or not '0'. This prevents proper word extraction. It should be `type(c) == 'a'` to continue reading letters.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This condition is always true because a character cannot be both 'a' and '0' type at the same time. It should break when the character is NOT alphabetic, but current logic breaks on any character including letters. Also, digits should not be included in words per problem definition.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [The condition `type(c) != 'a' || type(c) != '0'` is always true because any character is either not 'a' or not '0'. This causes premature break and incorrect word extraction. It should be `type(c) == 'a'` to continue reading letters only.]
